title: GENERAL STANDARD FOR CANNED MIXED FRUITS

sections.section: GENERAL STANDARD FOR CANNED MIXED FRUITS
sections.summary: Document title and identifier: General Standard for Canned Mixed Fruits (CXS 361‑2020), adopted in 2020.
Document identifier and adoption year heading only (CXS 361-2020 adopted in 2020); non‑substantive.

Applies to canned mixed fruits offered for direct consumption (including catering and repackaging) and to specific products in annexes, but not when intended for further processing; excludes products labeled for special dietary uses, reduced- or very-low-sugar products, and products where sweetening foodstuffs are wholly or partly replaced by sweetener additives, with general provisions applying unless otherwise specified.
Section heading introducing the product description and the forthcoming product‑definition subsection; non‑substantive by itself.

Defines canned mixed fruits as mixtures of whole small fruits and/or small pieces (subject to normal culinary preparatory operations) packed with a suitable liquid packing medium or vacuum‑packed (packing medium ≤20% net weight and internal pressure per GMP) and thermally or otherwise appropriately processed before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage and ensure ambient‑temperature stability.

Defines 'style' as the nature of the fruit combination (e.g., 2 fruits, tropical) and requires that annex-covered products only use styles permitted in the annex, while non‑annex products may use any style that meets the general standard and Section 2.2.1.

For non‑annex products any presentation is permitted provided it is sufficiently distinctive from other prescribed forms and is clearly described on the label to avoid consumer confusion or misleading information.
Varietal type for annexed products must follow the individual annexes, while non‑annex products may use any commercial variety or type suitable for canning.

Section heading introducing essential composition and quality factors, indicating the standard will address ingredients, packing media and quality criteria (including a note on high vacuum conditions); substantive requirements are provided in the subsequent subsections.
Subsection heading for composition (leads into basic and optional ingredients and packing media); non‑substantive by itself.
Specifies the basic ingredients are the fruits defined in Section 2 (and annexes where applicable) and an appropriate liquid packing medium as per Section 3.1.3.

Allows optional ingredients per individual annexes for annex products; for non‑annex products optional ingredients may include (but are not limited to) spices and culinary herbs, spice oil, and aloe vera.

Requires packing media to follow the Guidelines for packing media for canned fruits (CXG 51-2003) and permits clarified pineapple juice; the cut‑out strength for syrup packing media is determined on average, and no container may have a soluble solids (°Brix) value beyond the next category for that medium.
Section heading for quality criteria introducing organoleptic and other quality requirements; substantive provisions appear in the following subsections.

Canned mixed fruits must have normal colour, flavour and odour appropriate to the fruit types, packing medium and optional ingredients used, and must possess the texture characteristic of the product.
For annex products, fruit proportions must follow individual annex provisions; for non‑annex products any proportions not specified in annexes are permitted.
Size and shapes of fruits must comply with individual annex provisions for annex products, and for non‑annex products should be appropriate for the product.
For non‑annex products canned mixed fruits should be substantially free from defects, while for annex products defects must not exceed the limits fixed in the individual annexes.
Defines a 'defective' as any container failing to meet one or more applicable quality requirements in Section 3.2, excluding requirements based on sample averages.

A lot meets applicable quality requirements when non‑average requirements have 'defectives' not exceeding the sampling plan's acceptance number (c) at an AQL of 6.5, and requirements based on sample averages are complied with.

Permits only listed food additive classes for annex products and allows listed or otherwise justified additive classes for non‑annex products; acidity regulators, antioxidants and firming agents may be used in accordance with the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 04.1.2.4 and related tables, and flavourings must comply with the Guidelines for the use of flavourings (CXG 66-2008).

sections.section: 4.2 Flavourings used in products covered by this standard (i.e., products covered by annexes and products not covered by annexes) should comply with the Guidelines for the use of flavourings (CXG 66-2008).
sections.summary: Flavourings used in the products covered by this standard (both annex and non-annex products) must comply with the Codex Guidelines for the use of flavourings (CXG 66-2008).
The products must comply with Codex limits for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995) and with maximum residue limits for pesticides as established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.section: 5.1 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General standard for contaminants and toxins in food and feed (CXS 193-1995). 4
sections.summary: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels set out in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.section: 5.2 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.summary: The products shall comply with the Codex Alimentarius Commission's maximum residue limits for pesticides.

It is recommended that products be prepared and handled per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for canned fruit and vegetable products (CXC 2-1969) and other relevant Codex codes, and that they comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

sections.section: 6.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 5 Code of hygienic practice for canned fruit and vegetable products (CXC 2-1969), 6  and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
sections.summary: Products covered by this standard are recommended to be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for canned fruit and vegetable products (CXC 2-1969) and other relevant Codex texts.

sections.section: 6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and guidelines for the establishment and application of microbiological criteria related to foods (CXG 21-1997).
sections.summary: The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

Weights and measures rules require containers to meet minimum fill and drained-weight standards (minimum fill generally 90% of water capacity with exceptions), classify containers failing fill as 'defective', apply lot-acceptance sampling with an AQL of 6.5, and set minimum drained-weight provisions (annex-specific or 50% for non-annex products).

Containers should be filled so the product (including packing medium) occupies not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (minus necessary headspace); this does not apply to vacuum-packaged fruits, and flexible or rigid plastic containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.

The container must be well filled so the product including packing medium occupies at least 90% of the container's water capacity (less appropriate headspace), with exceptions for vacuum-packed fruits and special provisions for flexible and rigid plastic containers.
Any container that fails to meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 is to be classified as a 'defective'.
A lot meets the minimum-fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan evaluated at an AQL of 6.5.

For products covered by annexes, drained-weight requirements are set in the individual annexes; for products not covered, drained weight must be not less than 50% based on the weight of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

Minimum drained-weight requirements are deemed complied with when the average drained weight of all containers examined meets or exceeds the minimum required, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

Drained-weight provisions require annex-specified minima for annex products or a 50% minimum for non-annex products, and lot acceptance is based on the average drained weight meeting the minimum with no unreasonable shortages in individual containers.

Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and additionally must follow specific provisions on product name, size declaration, packing medium indication (including 'vacuum packaged' where applicable), style, flavouring declarations if added ingredients alter characteristic flavor, and optional varietal type.

sections.section: 8.1 The products covered by the provisions of this  standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985). 8  In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
sections.summary: The products covered by this standard must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with additional specific labelling provisions applicable.

For products covered by annexes, product names must be those defined in the annexes, while non-annex products may use specific names provided they are not misleading, and for annex products fruit sizes may be declared as part of the name or nearby.

sections.section: 8.2.1 For products covered by the annexes, the names of the canned mixed fruits shall be those defined in the individual annexes. For products not covered by the annexes, specific names of canned mixed fruits should be permitted provided that they are not misleading or confusing to the consumer.
sections.summary: For products covered by the annexes, when fruits are sized the size (or sizes, if mixed) as defined in the annexes may be declared as part of the product name or in close proximity to it.

sections.section: 8.2.2 For products covered by the annexes, when the fruits are sized, the size (or sizes when sizes are mixed), as defined in the individual annexes, may be declared as part of the name or in close proximity to the name of the product.
sections.summary: When fruits are sized for products covered by the annexes, the size(s) as defined in the annexes may be declared as part of the product name or in close proximity to it.

Sampling Plan 1 (Inspection level I, AQL = 6.5) prescribes sample sizes and acceptance numbers by lot-size for packages with net weight ≤ 1 kg (e.g., N≤4,800 → n=6, c=1; N>240,000 → n=60, c=7) under an AQL of 6.5 and indicates a separate category for net weights >1 kg up to 4.5 kg.
Heading stating that canned fruit cocktail is subject to the general provisions for canned mixed fruits plus the following specific provisions (introduces Section 1: Description).
Section heading introducing the product description (product definition and styles); not substantive by itself.
Defines canned fruit cocktail as a product prepared from a mixture of small fruits and small pieces of fruits listed in Section 1.3.
Section heading introducing product styles (e.g., five-fruit and four-fruit cocktails); not substantive by itself.
Defines the five-fruit fruit cocktail as a mixture of the five fruits listed in Section 1.3, with apples permitted as a substitute for pears.
Defines the four-fruit fruit cocktail as a mixture of four fruits from Section 1.3 with the option that either cherries or grapes may be omitted.

Specifies permitted species/varieties, required forms and notable characteristics for each fruit used: apples (Malus domestica, peeled/cored/diced, any cultivar), cherries (Prunus cerasus, halves or whole, pitted or unpitted; light sweet or may be artificially red-coloured with/without added flavouring), grapes (Vitis spp., whole seedless), peaches (Prunus persica, peeled/pitted/diced, firm yellow varieties excluding nectarines), pears (Pyrus spp., peeled/cored/diced, any variety), and pineapples (Ananas comosus, peeled/cored in sectors or diced).
Section heading for essential composition and quality factors (introduces composition and quality requirements); not substantive by itself.
Section heading for composition (introduces basic and optional ingredients); not substantive by itself.
States that the basic ingredients of canned fruit cocktail are the fruits defined in Section 1.
Permits optional ingredients such as spices and culinary herbs.

Sets quality criteria requiring normal colour (minor leaching from coloured cherries acceptable), normal flavour characteristic of the constituent fruits and any optional ingredients, and texture appropriate to each fruit (not excessively firm or soft).

Requires specific drained-weight proportions of fruits in the product: for 5-fruit cocktails peaches 30–50%, pears or apples 25–45%, pineapple 6–16%, grapes 6–20%, cherries 2–6%; for 4-fruit cocktails pineapple may be 6–25% and cherries up to 15% per the table.
Section heading introducing size and shape requirements for fruit pieces in the product; not substantive by itself.
Requires that at least 75% of drained diced peaches, pears/apples, or pineapple be approximately cube-shaped, not exceed 20 mm on the greatest edge, and not pass through 8 mm square meshes.
Requires that at least 80% of drained pineapple sectors approximate wedge shapes with outside arc 10–25 mm, thickness 10–15 mm, and radius 20–40 mm.
Requires that at least 90% by count of whole grapes or whole cherries approximate normal shape after proper preparation and not be broken into multiple parts or seriously crushed, mutilated, or torn.
Requires that at least 80% by count of cherry units be approximate halves and not broken into two or more parts (applies to halved cherry style).

Defines defects: blemished fruit pieces (dark surface areas, penetrating spots, other abnormalities); peel is a defect only when present on fruits that should be peeled; pit material includes pieces of pit or sharp pit points but excludes very small pit fragments under 5 mm without sharp points or edges.

For canned fruit cocktail, maximum defect allowances by drained fruit weight are: blemished fruit pieces up to 20% m/m; aggregate peel area up to 25 cm² per kg; pit material up to 1 piece per 2 kg; small stems up to 5 per kg; and large stems up to 1 per kg.
Minimum drained weight: the product's drained weight must be at least 60% of the weight of distilled water at 20°C that the sealed, completely filled container will hold.
Minimum drained weight: the drained weight of the product shall be not less than 60% of the weight of distilled water at 20°C which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.
Labelling requirement: the name of the product shall be 'fruit cocktail'.
The name of the product shall be 'fruit cocktail'.
Definition: canned tropical fruit salad is prepared from a mixture of the basic fruits listed in Section 1.3.1 and may include one or more optional fruits listed in Section 1.3.2.
Canned tropical fruit salad is the product prepared from a mixture of basic fruits (Section 1.3.1) to which one or more optional fruits (Section 1.3.2) may be added.

Style requirement: the fruit ingredient shall include fruits from each of the two fruit groups listed under Section 1.3.1 'Basic fruits' and may include any one or more optional fruits from Section 1.3.2.
Varietal type heading introducing basic fruits (Section 1.3.1) such as pineapple and related specifications for forms (e.g., chunks, tidbits, pieces, dices).
Lists basic fruits permitted in the product, including pineapple (Ananas comosus) in chunks/tidbits/pieces/dices and papaya (Carica papaya) in pieces, dices or slices.

Optional fruits: any suitable tropical fruits may be used and a non‑exhaustive table specifies permitted optional fruits (e.g., banana, carambola, cashew, dragon fruit, grapefruit, grape, guava, jackfruit, litchi, longan, mango, melon, orange, passionfruit, pomegranate, rambutan, watermelon) with their scientific names and allowed forms (slices, dices, chunks, segments, arils, pulp, etc.).
Composition: the product's basic ingredients are the tropical fruits defined in Sections 1.3.1 and 1.3.2.
Composition: tropical fruits as defined in Sections 1.3.1 and 1.3.2 comprise the product's basic ingredients.
Basic ingredients: the product shall contain the tropical fruits specified in Sections 1.3.1 and 1.3.2.

Optional ingredients allowed include aloe vera, nata de coco (coconut jelly/gel), purees of tropical fruits, tropical fruit juices, spices and culinary herbs (including spice oils), and cherries (Prunus cerasus) which may be light sweet, artificially coloured red, or artificially coloured red with added flavourings.

Quality criteria: canned tropical fruit salad must have colour characteristic of the mixed processed fruits (slight bleaching from coloured cherries or packing juices like pomegranate or passionfruit is acceptable), a normal flavour and odour for the blend, and texture appropriate to each fruit.

Sets quality requirements for colour (characteristic of the mixed fruits with slight bleaching from coloured cherries/packing juices acceptable), flavour and odour (normal for the blend), and texture (appropriate for each fruit).

Colour requirement: the product shall have a colour characteristic of the mixed processed fruits, with slight bleaching from coloured cherries or packing juices (e.g., pomegranate, passionfruit) allowed.
Flavour and odour requirement: canned tropical fruit salad shall have a normal flavour and odour characteristic of the particular blend of fruits.
Texture requirement: the texture of the fruit ingredient shall be appropriate for the respective fruit.
Specifies required proportions by drained fruit weight for the product: pineapple 20–70%, papaya 20–70%, and optional fruits 5–20%.
